title: Nursery Rhyme. CLVIII. Songs.
content:
Merry are the bells, and merry would they ring,
        Merry was myself, and merry could I sing;
        With a merry ding-dong, happy, gay, and free,
        And a merry sing-song, happy let us be!

        Waddle goes your gait, and hollow are your hose,
        Noddle goes your pate, and purple is your nose;
        Merry is your sing-song, happy, gay, and free,
        With a merry ding-dong, happy let us be!

        Merry have we met, and merry have we been,
        Merry let us part, and merry meet again;
        With our merry sing-song, happy, gay, and free,
        And a merry ding-dong, happy let us be!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CLVIII. Songs.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
